mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, lowering present values and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window signals deteriorating labor conditions and reduced consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real sales of final goods and housing starts indicates synchronized demand growth, which supports corporate earnings and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60d returns with statistical significance (t-stat < 1.5) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month contraction in the index of industrial production below negative 0.5 percent signals immediate supply bottlenecks and inventory drawdowns, triggering risk-off sentiment and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of contraction instances following the threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation runs persistently above 3 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 2 percent, real rates turn deeply negative, stimulating equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative real rate environment yields flat or negative 20d returns in > 55% of cases, contradicting the valuation expansion claim.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue expansion, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between GDPC1 growth and 60d returns is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) over 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in total nonfarm payrolls exceeding 50000 jobs signals abrupt labor market weakening and reduced wage growth, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Market rallies in > 60% of instances following large payroll declines, negating the labor weakening signal.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production expands while real sales of final goods contract indicates inventory accumulation and potential future production cuts, generating negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence pattern fails to predict negative 5d returns with accuracy exceeding 50% over a 3-year out-of-sample period.
